description_long: This triumphal arch is a rectangular stone structure with a central arched passageway and two smaller side arches, reminiscent of historic city gates. The facade features a large, prominent arch at the center, flanked by smaller arched openings and topped with a solid, blocky upper section. The surface is textured to resemble light-colored, weathered stone, with subtle brickwork patterns visible throughout. Decorative elements are minimal, with only a few small window-like openings and a scalloped detail above the main arch. The rear side is simpler, with arched windows and less ornamentation. The overall form is massive and monumental, evoking the style of medieval or early modern European city gates.
description: A monumental stone triumphal arch with a large central archway, flanked by smaller arches and minimal decorative detailing. The structure is blocky, textured like weathered stone, and evokes historic city gates.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blocky profile and the protruding upper section, with minimal visible openings.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main central archway, two smaller side arches, and a scalloped decorative detail above the main arch.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the blocky profile and upper section, with no major openings visible.
description_view_3: Rear view with two arched windows and a central archway, lacking the scalloped detail seen on the front.
